title: Share of countries by gender of the head of state
subtitle: The head of state is the chief public representative of their country.
note: This is recorded on December 31 of each year.
caption:
Share of countries by gender of the head of state

The head of state is the chief public representative of their country.

This stacked area chart displays the global share of countries led by a man versus a woman from 1789 to 2025, with data recorded on December 31 of each year. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 100%. The chart is dominated by a large beige area representing countries where a man is the head of state, which occupies nearly the entire vertical space throughout the timeline. A thin purple band at the bottom represents the share of countries where a woman is the head of state. From 1789 through the mid-20th century, this purple band remains very close to the 0% baseline, with only minor fluctuations and small peaks that do not exceed 10%. Starting around the 1960s, the purple area begins a gradual but visible upward trend. By the year 2000, the share of countries with a female head of state reaches approximately 5% to 8%. In the most recent years shown, approaching 2025, the purple band widens further, reaching its highest point on the chart at roughly 10% to 12%. Consequently, the beige area representing male heads of state contracts slightly in recent decades but still accounts for approximately 88% to 90% of the total share. The visual contrast highlights that while the proportion of female heads of state has increased over time, particularly in the last 60 years, men have consistently held the vast majority of these positions globally across the entire period.
